paper_title: Analysis of Biomass and Organotin Degradation

methods.description: Statistical data from the 30 day pot trial was used to investigate the total dry biomass of Hordeum vulgare and to test hypotheses and interactions related to bacterial inoculation and environment type in non-contaminated pots.

steps.description: A two way analysis of variance was used to investigate the total dry biomass of Hordeum vulgare and to test the following hypothesis and interactions: A null hypothesis, (there is no difference between seedlings of Hordeum vulgare inoculated or not inoculated with bacteria for total dry biomass) and B null hypothesis, (there is no difference between soil or sediment pots for total biomass of Hordeum vulgare). In addition the null hypothesis C states (there is not a significant interaction between the bacteria inoculums and the type of environment).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform two-way ANOVA on total dry biomass data from non-contaminated pots
inputs: Total dry biomass data of Hordeum vulgare
Factor A: Bacteria inoculum (C7, C22, non-inoculated)
outputs: ANOVA results (p-values for Factor A, Factor B, and interaction)

methods.description: A two way analysis of variance was used to analyse the data from the total dry biomass of Hordeum vulgare in contaminated sediment (1, 10 and 50 mg kg -1 TBT) to test hypotheses related to TBT concentration and bacterial inoculation.

steps.description: A two way analysis of variance was also used to analyse the data from the total dry biomass of Hordeum vulgare in contaminated sediment 1, 10 and 50 mg kg -1 respectively to test the following hypothesis: A null hypothesis, (TBT polluted sediment 1, 10 and 50 mg kg -1 will have no effect on the biomass of Hordeum vulgare) and B null hypothesis, (bacteria inoculum will have no effect on the biomass of Hordeum vulgare in TBT polluted sediment).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform two-way ANOVA on total dry biomass data from contaminated pots
inputs: Total dry biomass data of Hordeum vulgare from contaminated pots
Factor A: TBT concentration (1, 10, 50 mg kg -1)
Factor B: Bacteria inoculum (C7, C22, non-inoculated)
outputs: ANOVA results (p-values for Factor A and Factor B)

methods.method_name: Sediment Analysis by GC-MS for Organotins
methods.description: Analysis of sediment samples collected from roots of harvested seedlings and control pots by Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry (GC-MS) to identify and quantify TBT, DBT, and MBT, including sample preparation steps like extraction and derivatization.

steps.description: Sediment samples collected from roots of harvested seedlings Hordeum vulgare and sediment from control pots were analysed by GC-MS to determine TBT degradation rate. Also the degradation products dibutyltin (DBT) and monobutyltin (MBT).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment samples from plant roots and control pots

steps.description: Optimum GC and detector conditions were established.
steps.searchable_summary: Optimize GC-MS parameters for organotin analysis

steps.description: Accurate sample preparation including, derivatisation and liquid-liquid extraction to increase separation, identification and quantification.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform liquid-liquid extraction and derivatization on sediment samples

steps.description: An analytical procedure for separation and identification of organotin compounds was optimised on standard samples ( Figure 7) and then compared to sediment samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Optimize and validate analytical procedure using organotin standards

steps.description: The GC-MS was operated in full scan mode and selective ion monitoring mode (SIM) which allowed for detection of specific analysts and increased sensitivity relative to full scan mode. As such in SIM mode the instrument is set to identify only masses of interest (TBT, DBT and MBT) instead of all masses of the sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze prepared sediment samples using GC-MS in full scan and SIM mode

steps.description: Qualitative analysis was carried out comparing peak retention time (RT) of standard solutions against peak retention times of sediment samples (Table 1). All so a comparison was made of the ion trap mass spectra of the root/ sediment samples against the standard ion trap mass spectra, from the NIST reference Mass Spectrometry Library.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform qualitative analysis of GC-MS data using retention time and mass spectra comparison
inputs: GC-MS data (chromatograms, mass spectra) from samples
GC-MS data (chromatograms, mass spectra) from standards
outputs: Identification of organotin compounds (TBT, DBT, MBT) in samples

steps.description: Quantitative analysis was carried out on the (ethylderivatives) of TBT, DBT and MBT in sediment samples removed from the roots of Hordeum vulgare seedlings with or without bacteria inoculum (n=15), measuring peak area from the 3 distinctive peak in the chromatographs.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify organotin compounds in sediment samples using peak area measurement
inputs: Identified peaks for ethyl-derivatives of TBT, DBT, MBT
outputs: Peak areas for TBT, DBT, MBT in samples

steps.description: Five point calibration curves were generated using triplicate injections of 1µl of TBT, DBT and MBT standards which had good linearity with R 2 values of 0.999245 for MBT, 0.99919 for DBT and 0.991232 for TBT.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate calibration curves for TBT, DBT, and MBT standards
inputs: Standard solutions of TBT, DBT, MBT (five concentrations)
GC-MS peak areas from triplicate injections of standards
outputs: Calibration curves (linear models, R^2 values) for TBT, DBT, MBT

steps.description: Additionally the following limits of quantification (LOQ) were obtained 0.5, 0.7, and 0.8 mg/kg for TBT, DBT and MBT respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine limits of quantification (LOQ) for organotin analysis
outputs: LOQ values for TBT, DBT, MBT

steps.description: Results show sediment samples with bacteria inoculum C7/C22 decreased TBT by 82% and 80% respectively (Figure 9). In addition root sediment samples (10 mg/kg) containing Hordeum vulgare + C22/C7 inoculum showed approximately a 73.5% degradation of TBT with a 19%, accumulation of DBT and 37% accumulation of MBT (Figure 9).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate TBT degradation rates and degradation product accumulation percentages
inputs: Quantified concentrations of TBT, DBT, MBT in sediment samples (with/without bacteria inoculum, different initial TBT concentrations)
